e.def_cat: Cobrir una cosa d'aigua o un altre líquid.

cat.variants: anar amb un pam de llengua fora

e.def_eng: To fill with alarm or horrify.
e.def_cat: Omplir d'alarma, preocupació o horror.

example.sent_eng: The news dismayed us all.
example.sent_cat: La notícia ens va esfereir.

e.def_eng: To depress or lose courage.
e.def_cat: Abatre l'ànim o el coratge.

example.sent_eng: Don't let the failure dismay you.
example.sent_cat: No deixis que el fracàs et desanimi.

e.def_eng: To disquiet or cause sudden shock.
e.def_cat: Torbar o causar un xoc sobtat.

example.sent_eng: She was dismayed by the sudden change.
example.sent_cat: El canvi inesperat la va consternar.

e.def_eng: To burst into flames.
To make something burn.
e.def_cat: Fer que alguna cosa cremi.

example.sent_eng: She reignited the fire with dry wood.
example.sent_cat: Va tornar a encendre el foc amb llenya seca.

example.sent_eng: The news reignited the debate.
example.sent_cat: La notícia va reobrir el debat.

example.sent_eng: The news reignited the debate.
example.sent_cat: La notícia va tornar a encendre el debat.

example.sent_eng: revive old passions
example.sent_cat: revifar velles passions

example.sent_eng: reignite hopes
example.sent_cat: revifar les esperances

example.sent_eng: reignite the project
example.sent_cat: reactivar el projecte

example.sent_eng: They reignited her artistic career.
example.sent_cat: Van rellançar la seva carrera artística.

example.sent_eng: These measures will stimulate the economy.
example.sent_cat: Aquestes mesures dinamitzaran l'economia.

example.sent_eng: Human activities continue to anthropize pristine natural habitats.
example.sent_cat: Les activitats humanes continuen antropitzant hàbitats naturals verges.

example.sent_eng: The valley has been deeply anthropized due to agricultural development.
example.sent_cat: La vall ha estat profundament humanitzada a causa del desenvolupament agrícola.

example.sent_eng: He managed to quit smoking for a year, but then he slipped back.

example.sent_eng: It's easy to slip into bad habits during the holidays.
example.sent_cat: És fàcil caure en mals costums durant les vacances.

example.sent_eng: The patient slipped into a coma after the accident.

example.sent_eng: She managed to slip in a clever remark during the meeting.

example.sent_cat: escapolir-se avergonyit

example.sent_eng: to slip away unnoticed
example.sent_cat: esmunyir-se sense que ningú ho noti

example.sent_eng: to slip off quietly

example.sent_eng: The afternoon slipped away.

example.sent_eng: Our chances are slipping away.

example.sent_eng: She crocheted a doily.

example.sent_eng: He began to meander during his speech.

example.sent_eng: They were meandering through the park.

example.sent_eng: The river meanders through the valley.

example.sent_eng: Workers are being dragooned into using AI.
example.sent_cat: Forcen els treballadors a fer servir IA.

example.sent_eng: They were dragooned into signing the unfair contract.
example.sent_cat: Els van forçar a signar el contracte injust.

example.sent_eng: Our parents drilled honesty into us from a young age.
example.sent_cat: Els nostres pares ens van inculcar l'honestedat des de ben petits.

example.sent_eng: We used dry leaves to kindle the campfire.
example.sent_cat: Vam usar fulles seques per a encendre la foguera.

example.sent_eng: The damp hay in the barn began to kindle spontaneously.
example.sent_cat: El fenc humit del paller es va començar a encendre espontàniament.

example.sent_eng: The speaker's words kindled the crowds' anger.
example.sent_cat: Les paraules de l'orador van atiar la ràbia de la multitud.

example.sent_eng: Seeing the old photographs kindled long-forgotten memories.
example.sent_cat: Veure les fotografies velles li va revifar records oblidats de feia temps.

e.def_eng: To call forth emotions, feelings and responses.
e.def_cat: Provocar emocions, sentiments i respostes.

example.sent_eng: Her performance kindled a deep sense of nostalgia in the audience.
example.sent_cat: La seva actuació va despertar un profund sentiment de nostàlgia en el públic.

example.sent_eng: The accountant managed to embezzle thousands of dollars from the company.
example.sent_cat: El comptable va aconseguir malversar milers de dòlars de l'empresa.

example.sent_eng: He was accused of misappropriating public funds.
example.sent_cat: El va acusar de sostreure fons públics.

example.sent_eng: The corrupt politician pocketed the aid money.
example.sent_cat: El polític corrupte es va embutxacar els diners de l'ajuda.

example.sent_eng: He stashed away the cash and embezzled all he could.
example.sent_cat: Va guardar els efectius i va arrambar tot el que va poder.

example.sent_eng: She stashed away some food in case of emergency.
example.sent_cat: Va guardar una mica de menjar per si de cas hi havia una emergència.

example.sent_eng: He spends all his time hoarding old newspapers.
example.sent_cat: Passa tot el temps acumulant diaris vells.

example.sent_eng: Over the years, they stashed away enough money for a trip.
example.sent_cat: Amb els anys, van arraconar prou diners per a un viatge.

example.sent_eng: She salted away a part of her salary every month.
example.sent_cat: Arraconava una part del sou cada mes.

example.sent_eng: She stashed away a part of her salary every month to buy a car.
example.sent_cat: Cada mes feia racó d'una part del sou per a comprar-se un cotxe.

example.sent_eng: The request has timed out.

example.sent_cat: La connexió ha superat el temps màxim.

example.sent_eng: The session timed out.
example.sent_cat: La sessió ha caducat.

example.sent_cat: deixa de perdre el temps

example.sent_eng: don't screw around
example.sent_cat: no perdis el temps

example.sent_eng: Hurry up, don't dawdle.
example.sent_cat: Afanya't, no t'entretinguis.

example.sent_eng: Don't even bother.
example.sent_cat: Ni et prenguis la molèstia.

example.sent_eng: He didn't bother to ask
example.sent_cat: No es va dignar a preguntar.

example.sent_eng: Don't bother to reply
example.sent_cat: No et prenguis la molèstia de respondre.

example.sent_eng: Don't bother with that.
example.sent_cat: No t'entretinguis amb això.

example.sent_eng: Why bother about it?
example.sent_cat: Per què capficar-se per això?

example.sent_eng: not to bother to explain
example.sent_cat: no voler perdre el temps en explicacions

example.sent_eng: The goalkeeper tried to waste time.
example.sent_cat: El porter va intentar perdre temps.

example.sent_eng: He loves to pontificate about politics at family dinners.
example.sent_cat: Li encanta pontificar sobre política en els sopars familiars.

example.sent_eng: The politician bloviated for an hour without answering the question.
example.sent_cat: El polític va doctorejar durant una hora sense respondre a la pregunta.

example.sent_eng: She needed to rant about her terrible day at the office.
example.sent_cat: Necessitava desfogar-se per la seva terrible jornada a l'oficina.

example.sent_eng: He keeps ranting on about the new taxes.
example.sent_cat: No para d'exclamar-se criticant els nous impostos.

example.sent_eng: The workers sounded off about the poor safety conditions.
example.sent_cat: Els treballadors van expressar-se enèrgicament sobre les males condicions de seguretat.

example.sent_eng: Don't patronize me; I know perfectly well how to change a tire.
example.sent_cat: No em tractis amb condescendència; sé perfectament com es canvia una roda.

example.sent_eng: She felt patronized by the way the doctor spoke to her.
example.sent_cat: Es va sentir tractada com a una criatura per la manera com li parlava el metge.

example.sent_eng: Local residents are encouraged to patronize small shops in the neighborhood.
example.sent_cat: S'anima els residents locals a ser clients de les petites botigues del barri.

example.sent_eng: He refused to patronize that restaurant after the bad service.
example.sent_cat: Es va negar a tornar a freqüentar aquell restaurant després del mal servei.

example.sent_eng: The royal family has patronized the arts for centuries.
example.sent_cat: La família reial ha fet de mecenes de les arts durant segles.

example.sent_eng: Several large corporations sponsor the exhibition.
example.sent_cat: Diverses grans corporacions patrocinen l'exposició.

example.sent_eng: He swore before the court that he was innocent.
example.sent_cat: Va jurar davant del tribunal que era innocent.

example.sent_eng: The lawyer averred that her client had a solid alibi.
example.sent_cat: L'advocada va asseverar que el seu client tenia una coartada sòlida.

example.sent_eng: She continued to assert her innocence despite the evidence.
example.sent_cat: Va continuar afirmant la seva innocència malgrat les proves.

example.sent_eng: The mayor alleged that the cost overruns were unavoidable.
example.sent_cat: El batlle va sostenir que els sobrecostos eren inevitables.

example.sent_eng: We need to verify the data before publishing it.
example.sent_cat: Cal que verifiquem les dades abans de publicar-les.

example.sent_eng: The judge ordered the authorities to release the prisoner immediately.
example.sent_cat: El jutge va ordenar a les autoritats excarcerar el presoner immediatament.

example.sent_eng: They will let him out after the trial.
example.sent_cat: El van excarcerar després del judici.

e.def_eng: To preserve the memory of someone or something forever, especially through a monument, ritual, or art.

example.sent_eng: The statue was built to memorialize the fallen soldiers.
example.sent_cat: L'estàtua es va construir per a perpetuar el record dels soldats caiguts.

example.sent_eng: The poet immortalized his lover in a famous sonnet.
example.sent_cat: El poeta va immortalitzar la seva estimada en un sonet famós.

example.sent_eng: In his speech, the old man remembered the days of his youth.
example.sent_cat: En el discurs, el vell va rememorar els dies de la seva joventut.

e.def_cat: Retre culte, respecte o reconeixement públic a les virtuts o els mèrits d'algú.

example.sent_eng: The city decided to honor the veteran artist.
example.sent_cat: La ciutat va decidir honorar l'artista veterà.

example.sent_eng: We are here to honor his memory.
example.sent_cat: Som aquí per a retre homenatge a la seva memòria.

e.def_cat: Fer efectiu allò a què hom s'havia compromès o obligat.

example.sent_eng: You must honor your wedding vows.
example.sent_cat: Has de complir els teus vots matrimonials.

example.sent_eng: They refused to honor the contract.
example.sent_cat: Van refusar complir el contracte.

e.def_eng: To conform to or accept the authority of a law, rule, or agreement.

example.sent_eng: Both nations agreed to honor the ceasefire.
example.sent_cat: Totes dues nacions van acordar respectar l'alto el foc.

example.sent_eng: Citizens must honor the laws of the country.
example.sent_cat: Els ciutadans han d'acatar les lleis del país.

e.def_cat: Celebrar formalment la memòria d'un fet o d'una persona mitjançant un acte públic o una cerimònia.

example.sent_eng: The town will mark the centenary of the library next week.
example.sent_cat: La ciutat commemorarà el centenari de la biblioteca la setmana que ve.

example.sent_eng: The museum will honor the anniversary of the revolution.
example.sent_cat: El museu commemorarà l'aniversari de la revolució.

example.sent_eng: to mark the occasion
example.sent_cat: per a commemorar l'esdeveniment

e.def_cat: Cobrar un preu excessiu, abusiu o desorbitat a algú per un servei o producte.

example.sent_eng: Tourists are always ripped off in that area.

example.sent_eng: They really ripped us off for the car repair.

e.def_cat: Fer sortir una cosa del seu lloc estirant-la o aplicant-hi força.

example.sent_eng: He yanked the old poster off the wall.
example.sent_cat: Va arrencar el pòster vell de la paret.

e.def_cat: Enganyar algú amb astúcia o artificis per treure'n un profit econòmic o un avantatge.

example.sent_eng: An online fraudster tried to cheat him out of his savings.

e.def_eng: To copy or plagiarize someone else's work, ideas, or style.

example.sent_eng: The melody of their new song completely rips off an old classic.
example.sent_cat: La melodia de la seva nova cançó plagia per complet un clàssic antic.

e.def_cat: Separar una branca d'una planta per la seva unió amb el tronc per fer-ne un esqueix.

example.sent_eng: You should rip off a healthy branch to propagate the plant.

e.def_eng: To successfully acquire or obtain something desirable, often through effort or skill.

example.sent_cat: obtenir una bona col·locació

example.sent_cat: pescar una pubilla rica

e.def_eng: To go around or avoid a place by taking an alternative route.
e.def_cat: Fer marrada per no passar per un indret o envoltar una població.

example.sent_eng: The new highway bypasses the city.

example.sent_eng: to bypass the security system
example.sent_cat: saltar el sistema de seguretat

example.sent_eng: to bypass a technical obstacle
example.sent_cat: salvar un obstacle tècnic

e.def_eng: To ignore or avoid dealing with someone or something.
e.def_cat: Ignorar algú o desentendre's d'un assumpte o d'una persona.

example.sent_eng: They bypassed the manager and went to the director.
example.sent_cat: Van ignorar el gerent i van anar directament al director.

e.def_eng: To pass around an enemy position rather than attacking it directly.
e.def_cat: Passar de llarg o encerclar una posició enemiga sense atacar-la directament.

example.sent_eng: The troops bypassed the fortified town.
example.sent_cat: Les tropes van encerclar la ciutat fortificada.

example.sent_eng: The comet began to fragment.

example.sent_eng: to fish for compliments

e.def_eng: To reveal unintentionally.

example.sent_eng: to ring with a pencil
example.sent_cat: encerclar amb un llapis

example.sent_eng: I say we go to the beach!
example.sent_cat: Proposo que anem a la platja!

e.def_eng: To perceive the real intention or purpose of something false or misleading.
e.def_cat: Percebre la intenció o propòsit reals d'una cosa falsa o enganyosa.

e.def_eng: To be perceived as something, to be similar to something.
e.def_cat: Ésser percebut d'alguna manera, tenir semblança a alguna cosa.

cat.remark: exclusivament en 3a persona del singular del present d'indicatiu

example.sent_eng: He looks like a good person.
example.sent_cat: Sembla bona persona.

e.def_eng: To look physically similar to someone else.
e.def_cat: Ésser físicament similar a algú altre.

example.sent_eng: I see a future of conflict and inequality.
example.sent_cat: M'imagino un futur de conflictes i desigualtat.

example.sent_eng: Let's set up a meeting for next week.
example.sent_cat: Convoquem una reunió la setmana vinent.

e.def_eng: To establish in a place.
e.def_cat: Establir en un lloc.

e.def_eng: To separate and distribute different parts.
e.def_cat: Separar i distribuir diverses parts.

example.sent_eng: They had to stoke the fire during the night.
example.sent_cat: Hagueren de revifar el foc durant la nit.

e.def_eng: To build up a supply of something.
e.def_cat: Aplegar certa quantitat d'una cosa.

example.sent_eng: This vegetables will not sustain us for two weeks.
example.sent_cat: Aquesta verdura no ens sustentarà durant dues setmanes.

example.sent_eng: I took the liberty of changing your title without asking.
example.sent_cat: M'he permès de canviar-te el títol sense preguntar.

e.def_eng: To have negative consequences.
e.def_cat: Tenir conseqüències negatives.

e.def_eng: To make excessive demands on something.
e.def_cat: Exigir massa a alguna cosa.

e.def_eng: To make a charge against someone.
e.def_cat: Fer una acusasió a algú.

example.sent_eng: We set camp on a spot where the trees thinned out.
example.sent_cat: Vam muntar el campament en un indret on els arbres clarejaven.

example.sent_eng: She hates it when we tousle her hair.
example.sent_cat: Odia que li esbullem els cabells.

example.sent_eng: They railed from Barcelona to València.
example.sent_cat: Van anar en tren de Barcelona a València.

example.sent_eng: Let's go back 50 years.
example.sent_cat: Ara reculem cinquanta anys.

e.def_eng: To sexually stimulate one's penis.
e.def_cat: Estimular-se sexualment el penis.

example.sent_eng: The test worries me.
example.sent_cat: L'examen em neguiteja.
